en: Our main story tonight concerns healthcare, the industry that's done basically no innovating in the past 50 years when it comes to big rolls of paper you plop your bare arse on.
ko: 오늘 메인 스토리는 의료 산업이에요. 50년간 엉덩이 밑에 까는 종이 롤 빼고는 혁신이 없죠.

en: Specifically, we're going to be talking about Medicaid, the public insurance program that provides health coverage to people with low incomes.
ko: 구체적으로 메디케이드 얘기를 할 거예요. 저소득층 건강보험 공공 프로그램이에요.

en: It is massive, representing one out of every six dollars spent on healthcare in the U.S. And as of last year, at least, it covered more than 90 million people.
ko: 규모가 엄청나요. 미국 의료비 6달러 중 1달러를 차지하고 작년 기준 9천만 명 이상이 가입돼 있었어요.

en: And the reason I put that in the past tense is thanks to something called the Medicaid unwinding, that number has been plummeting and fast.
ko: 과거형을 쓴 이유는 메디케이드 해체 때문에 그 숫자가 빠르게 줄고 있거든요.

en: Hundreds of thousands of Americans are finding out they may be without all important Medicaid coverage.
ko: 수십만 미국인이 중요한 메디케이드 보장을 잃게 될 수 있다는 걸 알게 되고 있어요.

en: It's a process they're calling Medicaid unwinding. They basically said, hey, sorry, you don't have insurance.
ko: 메디케이드 해체라는 과정이에요. 미안한데 보험 없다고 한 거예요.

en: We took you off the books. They didn't even tell me that I was dropped. We have people that we have served for 15 to 20 years, and suddenly they're dropped off Medicaid.
ko: 명단에서 뺐어요. 빠졌다는 통보도 없었어요. 15~20년간 관리해온 사람들이 갑자기 빠졌어요.

en: It's true. People have been getting kicked off Medicaid.
